machine.name: DC Thermal Escalation Detector
machine.description: Monitors CPU temperature and load to detect thermal runaway. Implements the regular expression pattern NORMAL+ → WARM+ → HOT → CRITICAL+ → EMERGENCY → [alarm]. A concurrent SAFE+ sequence fires the reset signal whenever conditions return to normal, enabling RS flip-flop recovery.

regularExpression.escalation: NORMAL+ → WARM+ → HOT → CRITICAL+ → EMERGENCY → output[1,0]
regularExpression.combined: The escalation and recovery sequences run concurrently. SAFE+ fires [0,1] during nominal conditions (effectively resetting any downstream FF). EMERGENCY fires [1,0] at peak thermal stress (setting the FF). Between these extremes (WARM/HOT/CRITICAL), no output is written and the downstream FF retains its last state.
regularExpression.isInitialNote: NORMAL and SAFE are isInitial vectors. In CES, isInitial vectors implement A*A = A+: the isInitial property provides the self-loop (A*), but at least one match (A) is required before any nextVectorIds are activated. There is no mechanism to arm WARM or produce SAFE output without a matching sensor reading — therefore NORMAL* and SAFE* would be incorrect; the true semantics are NORMAL+ and SAFE+.

singleTransitionAssumption.scope: continuous sensor space — STA is not applicable as defined for binary vectors
singleTransitionAssumption.notes: The continuous-space analog of STA is 'gradual monotonic progression' — sensor values advance by bounded increments per step. The THRESHOLD comparator (with per-element tolerances) provides natural tolerance for sensor noise. The escalation sequence NORMAL+ → WARM+ → HOT → CRITICAL+ → EMERGENCY defines overlapping threshold bands; adjacent bands share boundary values so gradual progressions correctly advance through all stages. Abrupt jumps (e.g., direct NORMAL → EMERGENCY) would skip intermediate states and fail to fire intermediate actions — consistent with the design intent that requires observable escalation before triggering the alarm.

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="data-center".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning DCThermalEscalation matures.

rules.description: THERMAL EMERGENCY: Alarm signal [1,0] to DCCoolingControlFF at [1375:1377]
THERMAL SAFE: Reset signal [0,1] to DCCoolingControlFF at [1375:1377]

sequences.name: Thermal Escalation: NORMAL+ → WARM+ → HOT → CRITICAL+ → EMERGENCY

metadata.description: 5-stage thermal escalation using Kleene-plus operators on continuous sensor values. All isInitial states implement A*A = A+ semantics.

metadata.name: NORMAL
metadata.description: NORMAL+ (= NORMAL*NORMAL, A*A) — one or more normal readings required before WARM is armed. The isInitial property provides the self-loop (A*); the mandatory match (A) is what activates WARM via nextVectorIds. Zero NORMAL readings → WARM is never activated.
metadata.semantics: isInitial (A*A = A+): never deactivates; on each match fires nextVectorIds — activating WARM (and keeping NORMAL active via self-loop). At least one NORMAL match is required to prime WARM. This is A*A not A*.

metadata.name: WARM
metadata.description: WARM+ — one or more warm readings required before advancing to HOT
metadata.semantics: Non-initial transitional with self-loop: deactivates after match, re-activated by its own nextVectorId. Also activates HOT when matched.

metadata.name: HOT
metadata.description: HOT — single sustained hot reading; mandatory transition point before critical
metadata.semantics: Non-initial transitional (no self-loop): exactly one HOT observation advances to CRITICAL. Prevents false critical triggers from transient spikes.

metadata.name: CRITICAL
metadata.description: CRITICAL+ — one or more critical readings sustaining the high-temperature condition
metadata.semantics: Non-initial transitional with self-loop: must be observed at least once before EMERGENCY. Models a sustained critical condition rather than a transient spike.

metadata.name: EMERGENCY
metadata.description: EMERGENCY — thermal shutdown imminent; fires alarm output [1,0]
metadata.semantics: Final vector with output: stays active on match (fires alarm each cycle), deactivates on no-match (temperature drops below threshold).

metadata.description: THERMAL EMERGENCY: Alarm signal [1,0] to DCCoolingControlFF at [1375:1377]
metadata.action: DCCoolingControlFF will SET; emergency cooling activated

metadata.description: Fires reset signal [0,1] on every cycle that CPU temperature and load are within safe operating range (one or more times). Concurrently active with the escalation sequence. Enables downstream DCCoolingControlFF to RESET when conditions are safe. SAFE is isInitial: A*A = A+, so at least one matching sensor reading is required to produce any output.

metadata.name: SAFE
metadata.description: Thermal conditions within safe range — fires [0,1] reset signal on every cycle conditions are nominal (A*A = A+: at least one match required to produce output)
metadata.semantics: isInitial (A*A = A+): always active; fires [0,1] to [1375:1377] on each matching cycle. At least one safe sensor reading is required before any reset output is produced. SAFE* would be incorrect — it would imply output is possible without any sensor match.

metadata.description: THERMAL SAFE: Reset signal [0,1] to DCCoolingControlFF at [1375:1377]
metadata.action: DCCoolingControlFF will RESET; normal cooling sufficient

inputSequences.name: Gradual Thermal Escalation to Emergency
inputSequences.description: Complete escalation-and-recovery scenario demonstrating all 5 thermal stages

metadata.note: Outputs at [1375:1377] persist between writes. After step 10 [1,0], bytes [1375:1377] hold [1,0] until step 12 fires [0,1]. DCCoolingControlFF will read [1,0] on steps 10-11, then read [0,1] at step 12.

inputSequences.name: Abrupt Spike (Non-Escalating)
inputSequences.pattern: NORMAL → HOT → NORMAL (skips WARM — fails to trigger escalation)
inputSequences.description: Demonstrates that abrupt temperature spikes without gradual escalation cannot trigger the alarm. HOT requires WARM+ to have been active first.

expectedOutputs.step_2: no output — HOT not yet activated (WARM+ was never satisfied)

metadata.note: HOT vector is only activated after WARM has matched at least once. An abrupt jump from NORMAL to HOT bypasses the WARM+ requirement, preventing false alarms from transient spikes.
